title: Simple 1x12 Bench or Bedside Table
summary: A genuinely minimal 1x12 bench with jigsaw-cut leg shapes and a single side trim board tying it together — one of the smallest, fastest builds in this catalog.
description:
This is about as simple as a real piece of furniture gets — two 1x12 legs, a shelf, a top, and a pair of side trim boards, with all the visual interest coming from a jigsaw-cut leg profile rather than any added parts. Cut the bottom of each leg into a shape using a jigsaw, following a traced or measured curve rather than leaving the legs as plain rectangles — this is the one step that takes any real technique, and it's worth taking slowly since both legs need to end up matching.

Attach the shelf and top to the legs next, and **if you're using pocket holes, attach the top before the shelf** — the plan is specific about this order because once the shelf is in place, there's no longer room to fit a drill inside the cavity to reach the top's pocket holes. Nails and glue don't have this constraint and can go in either order.

The side support trim goes on last, running the full length of the piece from front to back — this single board is what actually ties the top, shelf, and both legs into one rigid unit, rather than four parts that happen to be fastened near each other. At this scale it works equally well as a slim bedside table or a small entry bench.

materials.name: Brad nails, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Shape the legs
steps.body: Use a jigsaw to cut a shape into the bottom of the two leg pieces, following the diagram or your own traced curve. Keep both legs matching.

steps.title: Attach the shelf and top
steps.body: Attach the top and shelf to the legs with 1-1/4"–2" nails and glue, or pocket holes if you have a jig — attach the top first if using pocket holes, since the shelf will block drill access afterward.
materials: Brad nails, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the side trim
steps.body: Attach the side support pieces to the top and legs with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, tying the whole piece together.
materials: Brad nails, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Small simple 1x12 bench with shaped legs
